items.text_raw: The initial report claimed Takaichi's secretary and others were involved in videos produced and posted for last October's Liberal Democratic Party presidential race and February's general election that criticized politicians Wow. Politicians criticizing and smearing other politicians. That's a new one. including calling Takaichi's leadership rival and now Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi "incapable." If that's the only quote from the videos that's being highlighted here, I presume that was the worst one in the videos. Please. Calling a rival politician "incapable" is hardly the Mother of All Scandals. In America, one political party repeatedly and insistently calls the leader of the other party a Nazi and a fascist. That's much worse than calling someone "incapable." At least calling someone "incapable" is highly unlikely to incite assassination attempts. This thing with these videos? Much ado over nothing, if you ask me.
items.translation_ja: 最初の報道では、高市首相の秘書らが、昨年10月の自民党総裁選と2月の総選挙向けに制作・投稿された、他の政治家を批判する動画に関与したとされていた。政治家が他の政治家を批判し、中傷するなんて。珍しいことじゃない（皮肉）。高市氏のライバルで現防衛大臣の小泉進次郎を「無能」と呼んだ、という内容も含まれていた。ここで強調されている動画の引用がそれだけなら、動画の中では一番ひどい言葉だったのだろう。まあ、お願いだから。ライバル政治家を「無能」と呼ぶことは、史上最大のスキャンダルどころじゃない。アメリカでは、ある政党がもう一方の党のリーダーを何度も執拗にナチスやファシストと呼ぶ。それの方が「無能」と呼ぶよりずっとひどい。少なくとも「無能」と呼んでも暗殺を煽る可能性は極めて低い。この動画騒動？ 私に言わせれば、大げさすぎる、取るに足らない話だ。
notes_ja: 「Wow.」「That's a new one.」「Please.」は文が途切れた感じの短い文で、皮肉・呆れ・「まあいい加減に」というトーンを出している。
「including calling...」は前の文にぶら下がる形で、口語では主語なしの断片が続くことがある。
items.standard_en: The initial report claimed that Takaichi's secretary and others were involved in videos produced and posted for last October's Liberal Democratic Party presidential race and February's general election that criticized politicians. Wow. Politicians criticizing and smearing other politicians—that's a new one—including calling Takaichi's leadership rival and now Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi "incapable." If that's the only quote from the videos being highlighted here, I presume that was the worst one in the videos. Please. Calling a rival politician "incapable" is hardly the Mother of All Scandals. In America, one political party repeatedly and insistently calls the leader of the other party a Nazi and a fascist. That's much worse than calling someone "incapable." At least calling someone "incapable" is highly unlikely to incite assassination attempts. This thing with these videos? It's much ado over nothing, if you ask me.

marked_terms.note_ja: 歴史的に極めて深刻な呼び方。政治批判でも軽く使わない。
強い侮辱・レッテル。丁寧な会話では避ける。

items.author: Quo Primum

items.text_raw: Seeing how she mishandles this kind of issues it is not surprising she fears the press and runs instead to social media.
items.translation_ja: こういう問題の扱い方が下手なのを見ていると、記者会見を避けてSNSに逃げ込むのも不思議じゃない。
notes_ja: 「this kind of issues」は文法的には「these kinds of issues」の方が自然。口語・ネット英語ではよくあるズレ。
「runs instead to」はやや不自然で、一般的には「runs to ... instead」や「turns to ... instead」が自然。
items.standard_en: Seeing how she mishandles these kinds of issues, it is not surprising that she fears the press and turns to social media instead.
items.author: virusrex

items.text_raw: Scrub, ‘politically inconvenient issues’ and replace it with what Sanaesan most liked has deemed to be the ‘deeply flawed, dangerous and self defeating agendas’ of the opposition. Politics 101. Making videos to discredit them? Even if it’s true, Oh the scandal. More details necessary though for this to be an actual. Anyone know what was in the vids? Sort of matters right? If the videos contained deliberate lies and falsehoods, like what the BBC did with their work on Trump, that would be worth taking a look. I doubt it’ll be anything like that though, but you never know. We just got the ‘politically inconvenient issues’ tagline… As always the opposition seems to be clutching at straws, having little to nothing of much value to offer. Trying to paint power as inherently corrupt. Yada Yada Yada. Crying about stuff they themselves do constantly. Bunshu stirring the pot as always for circulation. Same thing all over the world. Smear and repeat. Nihon no hidari, gambare! Find your own voice and vision and we will all listen.
items.translation_ja: 「政治的に都合の悪い問題」という表現は消して、代わりにサナエさんが最も好んで使う、野党の「深刻な欠陥があり、危険で、自滅的なアジェンダ」という言い回しを入れろ、という話だ。政治の基本中の基本。相手を貶める動画を作った？ たとえ事実でも、まあ大スキャンダルね。でもこれを本当のスキャンダルにするには、もっと詳細が必要だ。動画の中身、誰か知ってる？ それ、結構大事だよね？ もし動画に意図的な嘘や虚偽が含まれていたなら、BBCがトランプ関連でやったような件なら、調べる価値はある。でもそこまでではないと思う。まあ、どうなるかは分からないが。結局「政治的に都合の悪い問題」というキャッチフレーズだけが出てきた… いつものように野党は藁にもすがるように見える。出せるものがほとんどない。権力＝本質的に腐敗、と描こうとしている。まあまあ、その辺はいいよ。自分たちもいつもやっていることを文句言うだけ。文春がいつものように売上のために火に油。世界中どこでも同じ。中傷して繰り返すだけ。日本の左派、頑張れ！ 自分たちの声とビジョンを見つければ、みんな聞くよ。
notes_ja: 「Scrub」は編集用語で「（表現を）消す・差し替える」という意味。文頭だけの命令形で、メディア編集の比喩。
「for this to be an actual」は文が途中で切れており、「an actual scandal（本当のスキャンダル）」の省略と思われる。
items.standard_en: Scrub "politically inconvenient issues" and replace it with what Sanaesan most liked has deemed to be the "deeply flawed, dangerous and self-defeating agendas" of the opposition. Politics 101. Making videos to discredit them? Even if it's true—oh, the scandal. More details are necessary, though, for this to be an actual scandal. Does anyone know what was in the videos? That sort of matters, right? If the videos contained deliberate lies and falsehoods, like what the BBC did with their work on Trump, that would be worth taking a look. I doubt it'll be anything like that, though, but you never know. We just got the "politically inconvenient issues" tagline… As always, the opposition seems to be clutching at straws, having little to nothing of much value to offer—trying to paint power as inherently corrupt. Yada yada yada. Crying about stuff they themselves do constantly. Bunshu is stirring the pot as always for circulation. Same thing all over the world: smear and repeat. Nihon no hidari, gambare! Find your own voice and vision, and we will all listen.

marked_terms.note_ja: ここでは口語・編集用語。「消せ・差し替えろ」という意味。
videos の略。カジュアルなネット英語。
「まあまあ」「その辺はいいよ」というあきれた省略表現。
藁にもすがる、という慣用句。相手を見下すニュアンスになりやすい。

items.author: Ricky Kaminski13

items.text_raw: The real question should be, how was those smear recordings or videos obtained by the magazine running them?
items.translation_ja: 本当に問うべきは、それらの中傷録音や動画を、掲載した雑誌はどうやって入手したのか、ということだ。
notes_ja: 「how was those」は文法的に誤りで、複数なら「how were those」が正しい。
文全体は口語的な疑問文で、主語と動詞の一致が崩れている典型例。
items.standard_en: The real question should be: how were those smear recordings or videos obtained by the magazine running them?
items.author: Cephus

items.text_raw: An interesting report from Japanese media on political shenanigans! What are the facts?
items.translation_ja: 日本のメディアによる、政治の裏工作をめぐる興味深い報道だね。事実は何なんだ？
notes_ja: 「shenanigans」は「いたずら・不正な手口・裏工作」などのカジュアルな語。政治記事ではよく使われるが、やや口語的。
感嘆符付きの短い文＋疑問文という、コメント欄らしいテンポ。

marked_terms.note_ja: 口語的な語。政治の不正行為・裏工作を軽く言うときに使う。

items.author: kurisupisu
